preview.tournamentOverview: Aronimink Golf Club hosts the PGA Championship with a layout that places a premium on approach play and total strokes gained. The course demands precision iron play and consistent ball-striking over raw distance. This major carries extra weight as a key measuring stick for the season and a critical opportunity for players to claim a career-defining title.
preview.modelExplainer: Our model weights approach play heaviest at 30.7% and total strokes gained at 22.8%, making iron play and overall scoring the dominant factors. Off-the-tee contribution is secondary at 16.2%, while putting receives only 8.3% emphasis. Bogey avoidance and birdie conversion each carry 3.0% weight, underscoring the importance of minimizing mistakes on a course that punishes errant approaches.
preview.pickApproach: We tier players by combining tournament-specific ranks with baseline power rankings to balance course fit and overall ability. Higher-weighted stats like approach play and total SG drive early selections, while lower-weighted categories serve as tiebreakers. This structure prioritizes golfers who excel in the most predictive skills for Aronimink while maintaining a margin for course variance.
